stages.title: Performance audit — hangs, blocking ops, async issues
stages.description:
Perform a thorough performance audit of the entire Minions codebase (D:/squad). Read all core modules (engine.js, dashboard.js, engine/*.js, dashboard/*.js) and identify real performance problems.

Focus areas:
- **Hangs / blocking I/O**: synchronous fs calls (readFileSync, writeFileSync) on the hot path, blocking requires inside functions, synchronous JSON parsing of large files
- **Lack of optimistic updates**: dashboard API handlers that wait for a full file write before responding, work item mutations that could return immediately and confirm async, UI state that stalls on round-trips
- **Waiting for API**: serial `await` chains that could be `Promise.all`, ADO/GitHub polling that blocks the tick loop, agent live-stream handlers that await unnecessarily
- **Async operation issues**: unresolved promise chains, fire-and-forget mutations without error handling, missing concurrency limits on fan-out spawns, tick phases that don't yield between items
- **Lock contention**: mutateJsonFileLocked calls that hold locks too long or are called unnecessarily in read-only paths
- **SSE/streaming**: response streams not flushed promptly, missing heartbeat causing client timeouts, large payloads serialized synchronously before first byte sent

For each issue found, record:
- File and line number
- Category: hang / no-optimistic-update / api-wait / async-issue / lock-contention / sse
- Severity: critical / high / medium / low
- Description and how it manifests under load or in the UI
- Proposed fix

Produce a structured findings report. Do NOT make any code changes — analysis only.

stages.title: Performance Review — agree on fixes
stages.agenda:
Ripley has completed a performance audit of the Minions codebase. Review findings and agree on what to fix.

Findings:
{{stages.perf-audit.output}}

Discussion points:
1. Review each finding — is it a real problem or acceptable tradeoff?
2. For no-optimistic-update issues: agree on which API endpoints should return immediately vs. wait
3. For hangs and blocking ops: agree on whether to move to async, use streams, or cache
4. For async/await chains: agree on where Promise.all parallelisation is safe
5. Prioritise — critical and high severity first, limit scope to what can ship in one plan
6. Produce a concrete, ordered list of agreed fixes with rationale and file references

stages.title: Review meeting conclusions before planning
stages.description: Review the performance meeting conclusions above, then continue to create and dispatch the fix plan.

stages.title: Performance fix plan from weekly review
stages.description:
Based on the agreed performance fixes from the meeting:

{{stages.perf-meeting.output}}

Write a detailed implementation plan for the agreed performance improvements to the Minions codebase. Each item must:
- Reference the specific file(s) and function(s) to change
- Describe the before/after behaviour
- Be independently shippable (one PR per item where possible)
- Be ordered by severity (critical first)

The plan will be auto-approved and dispatched to agents immediately.
